global_rules: Learn from final user-edited posts more strongly than raw generated drafts.
Avoid exact private tenure details unless the manifest explicitly marks them as public-safe.
Prefer a strong headline or hook for management posts.
Make private-note posts generalizable to leaders, managers, operators, product, engineering, and data audiences.
Use safe public examples only when they are widely known public history and not adjacent to the private source context.
Do not reuse the same conceptual territory merely by changing wording.

management_reflection: Lead with a broad leadership thesis, not a diary-style update.
Company phase language is encouraged: building, scaling, tightening, simplifying, optimizing, reinventing.
Contrast modes of work when useful, such as exploration vs optimization.
A short public business example can strengthen the post when it illustrates the management lesson.
Hashtags may be broader and more numerous than conference posts, up to 15.
career_transition: Avoid repeating role saturation, succession, handover, and leaving-when-systems-run themes unless requested.
Focus on reusable leadership principles instead of personal chronology.
Keep identifying details out of the post.
leadership_lesson: Frame the lesson as a decision pattern leaders can recognize.
Use concrete but non-identifying operating language.
End with a genuine question that invites practitioner discussion.
ai_data_practitioner: Stay specific about implementation, architecture, evaluation, and operating constraints.
Prefer grounded observations from shipped work or conference evidence.
public_example_analysis: Use only widely known public examples.
Name the company or public leader only when the source is public history.
Tie the example back to the private-note lesson without implying any connection to the private organization.

why_it_worked: The headline makes the management thesis clear before the personal reflection.
Exact private tenure was generalized to a less identifying timeframe.
The post speaks to a wider leadership audience instead of only describing an internal transition.
Building, scaling, tightening, and simplifying create a reusable mental model.
The Steve Jobs and Apple example adds public, recognizable evidence without revealing private details.
The final question is specific enough to invite real discussion.
avoid_next_time: Do not say 8 years or eight years.
Do not over-focus on handover, succession, or single points of failure.
Do not make the post sound like a private resignation note.
